refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated performance requirement derivation, recording, and management system that translates worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints) into documented performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) with full traceability to source inputs and maintained history of baseline calibrations and updates.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks specificity on the derivation mechanism, traceability structure, and historical management. Refinement clarifies that the system must bridge worksite context to quantified performance targets, maintain audit trails, and support iterative calibration—critical for safety-critical systems and stakeholder confidence (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) performance targets are derived from documented worksite inputs via a defined algorithm or decision logic; (2) traceability matrix links each target to source constraint(s); (3) baseline history is recorded with timestamps and change justification; (4) acceptance criteria are met per system-level constraints.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept and parse worksite mission context inputs (site geometry, soil conditions, operational policies, safety plan constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall apply a derivation algorithm to map worksite context to performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins).
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall record and maintain a traceability matrix linking each performance target to its source worksite input(s).
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall maintain a versioned history of performance baselines and calibration updates with timestamps and change rationale.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide query and reporting interfaces to retrieve current and historical performance targets and traceability records.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance management database with embedded derivation rules engine, traceability schema, and versioning; accessed via REST API by operational and planning modules.
DP-B: Distributed performance target calculation across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules with eventual consistency synchronization to a central audit log.

DP-C: Hybrid approach: core derivation and traceability logic in a dedicated Performance Management service; Vehicle and Teleoperation modules query and cache targets locally; Safety Services validates and enforces safety margins.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid approach with dedicated Performance Management service (derivation, traceability, versioning) coupled to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services via well-defined query and validation interfaces. This balances centralized authority (single source of truth for targets and history) with distributed consumption (modules operate autonomously with cached targets) and safety integration (Safety Services enforces margin constraints).

potential_sources: Coupling between Performance Management service and Safety Services on safety margin validation—mitigated by explicit interface contract (SafetyAlert feedback loop).
Coupling between Vehicle/Teleoperation and Performance Management on target refresh frequency—mitigated by asynchronous query pattern and local caching.
Coupling between worksite input parsing and derivation algorithm—mitigated by schema versioning and backward compatibility.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C avoids tight coupling of derivation logic across multiple modules (DP-B risk) and avoids monolithic database bottleneck (DP-A risk). Decoupling is achieved via service boundaries and interface contracts.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context inputs are well-defined and can be parsed into a structured schema. Risk: Ambiguous or incomplete worksite data may lead to invalid target derivation. Mitigation: Validation rules and user feedback loops in input acceptance (CR-NGHE-005.1).

Assumption: Derivation algorithm is deterministic and can be versioned. Risk: Algorithm changes may invalidate historical comparisons. Mitigation: Maintain algorithm version in baseline history; support rollback.

Assumption: Safety Services can validate performance targets against safety constraints in real-time. Risk: Validation latency or conflicts may delay operations. Mitigation: Pre-validation during derivation; SafetyAlert interface for asynchronous feedback.

Assumption: Network connectivity between Performance Management service and Vehicle/Teleoperation is reliable. Risk: Offline operation or network partition may cause stale targets. Mitigation: Local caching with conflict resolution; periodic resynchronization.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the derivation algorithm specification (e.g., rule-based, ML-based, lookup table) and define the schema for worksite mission context inputs before detailed design. Establish interface contracts (query request/response formats, SafetyAlert payload structure) to enable parallel module development.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and a new Performance Management service. The Performance Management service (owned by System Engineer or Maintenance/Quality stakeholder) resides within NGHE Solution and provides derivation, recording, and management of performance requirements. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) connect NGHE Solution to worksite systems and operators.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Worksite mission context inputs (site geometry, soil conditions, operational policies, safety plan constraints) flow into NGHE Solution via Tasking interface; Performance Management service parses and ingests these inputs.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) and traceability/history records are reported out via Telemetry interface to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality dashboards.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Performance Management service queries Safety Services for safety margin constraints (inbound); Safety Services validates derived targets and returns validation status or alerts (outbound) via SafetyAlert interface.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Performance Management service communicates with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules over Network Link to distribute targets and receive operational feedback for calibration updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Performance Management service is a core system-level capability that must be integrated at the NGHE Solution boundary to ensure traceability, safety validation, and stakeholder visibility. Minimal diffusion: all derivation, recording, and management logic is localized within this module.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing tasks and reporting operational telemetry, not for deriving or managing performance requirements. Placing derivation logic in Vehicle would fragment the single source of truth and complicate safety validation. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operator control and situational awareness, not for performance requirement management. Derivation logic in Teleoperation would create inconsistency with Vehicle-autonomous operations. High diffusion risk.

Safety Services is responsible for safety validation and enforcement, not for performance requirement derivation or historical management. While Safety Services must validate targets, the derivation and recording logic should remain in Performance Management service (within NGHE Solution) to maintain separation of concerns. Moderate diffusion risk if derivation is placed here.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: System Engineer (primary) with collaboration from Maintenance/Quality (for calibration history and baseline management) and Safety Manager (for safety margin validation). Performance Management service is a system-level capability owned by System Engineer; Safety Services validation is owned by Safety Manager.
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Performance Management service (a new internal module within NGHE Solution) is the optimal containment boundary. It centralizes derivation, recording, and management logic; interfaces with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services via well-defined contracts; and maintains traceability and history as a single source of truth. This avoids diffusion across Vehicle and Teleoperation and preserves Safety Services' focus on validation and enforcement.
containment_risks: Risk: Performance Management service becomes a bottleneck if derivation algorithm is computationally expensive or if worksite input parsing is slow. Mitigation: Asynchronous processing, caching, and performance profiling during design.

Risk: Network latency between Performance Management service and Vehicle/Teleoperation may delay target distribution. Mitigation: Local caching with periodic resynchronization; offline fallback to cached targets.
Risk: Safety Services validation may conflict with derived targets, causing operational delays. Mitigation: Pre-validation during derivation; clear escalation and override procedures.
Risk: Historical data growth may impact query performance. Mitigation: Archival strategy, indexing, and database optimization.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define the internal architecture of Performance Management service (e.g., microservice, library, database schema) and establish explicit interface contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Clarify ownership and operational responsibilities (e.g., who manages calibration updates, who resolves validation conflicts).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (Hybrid Performance Management service) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) accepts worksite context via Tasking interface, (2) applies derivation algorithm, (3) maintains traceability matrix, (4) records versioned history, (5) provides query/reporting interfaces. The DP is logically sound and avoids unnecessary coupling. No design contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk (Low): If Performance Management service is not clearly bounded as a distinct module within NGHE Solution, derivation logic may leak into Vehicle or Teleoperation. Mitigation: Explicit module definition and interface contracts.

Diffusion Risk (Low): If traceability and history recording are duplicated across modules, audit trail integrity is compromised. Mitigation: Single source of truth in Performance Management service; other modules query only.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct containment boundary. Performance Management service (internal to NGHE Solution) achieves high containment (95%) with minimal diffusion (5%). External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and map to known interface names. No new interfaces required.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) Worksite input parsing can be tested with sample mission contexts. (2) Derivation algorithm can be validated against acceptance criteria (e.g., cycle time targets match expected ranges). (3) Traceability matrix can be audited for completeness and correctness. (4) Baseline history can be inspected for proper versioning and timestamps. (5) Query/reporting interfaces can be tested for accuracy and performance.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Performance Management service (within NGHE Solution) is responsible for all aspects of CR-NGHE-005. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are consumers of performance targets, not producers. Ownership is assigned to System Engineer with collaboration from Maintenance/Quality and Safety Manager.

recommended_revisions: Specify the derivation algorithm (rule-based, ML-based, lookup table) and document the schema for worksite mission context inputs before detailed design.
Define interface contracts (request/response formats, error handling, versioning) for Tasking (input), Telemetry (output), SafetyAlert (validation), and Network Link (distribution).
Establish operational procedures for calibration updates, baseline rollback, and conflict resolution between derived targets and safety constraints.
Plan for performance testing and scalability analysis (e.g., latency of derivation, query response time, historical data growth).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic; it comprises five distinct sub-FRs (input acceptance, derivation, traceability, history management, query/reporting). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define detailed DPs and IMs. The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass all gates, but the next level of functional decomposition is necessary to allocate responsibilities within Performance Management service and define detailed interface contracts.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept and parse worksite mission context inputs (site geometry, soil conditions, operational policies, safety plan constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall apply a derivation algorithm to map worksite context to performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins).
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall record and maintain a traceability matrix linking each performance target to its source worksite input(s).
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall maintain a versioned history of performance baselines and calibration updates with timestamps and change rationale.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide query and reporting interfaces to retrieve current and historical performance targets and traceability records.

assumptions: Worksite mission context inputs are well-defined and can be parsed into a structured schema.
Derivation algorithm is deterministic and can be versioned.
Safety Services can validate performance targets against safety constraints in real-time or near-real-time.
Network connectivity between Performance Management service and Vehicle/Teleoperation is reliable; offline operation is acceptable with local caching.
Performance targets are stable over the duration of a mission; frequent updates are not required.
Historical data retention requirements are defined (e.g., 1 year, 5 years, indefinite).
open_risks: Derivation algorithm specification is not yet finalized; algorithm changes may impact historical comparisons.
Worksite input schema may evolve; backward compatibility must be maintained.
Safety validation may conflict with derived targets; escalation and override procedures are not yet defined.
Performance Management service may become a bottleneck if derivation is computationally expensive or if worksite input parsing is slow.
Network latency or partition may cause stale targets in Vehicle/Teleoperation; conflict resolution strategy is not yet defined.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of derivation algorithm (rule-based, ML-based, lookup table) with examples.
Schema definition for worksite mission context inputs with validation rules.
Interface contracts (request/response formats, error handling, versioning) for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link.
Operational procedures for calibration updates, baseline rollback, and conflict resolution.
Performance requirements (latency, throughput, scalability) for Performance Management service.
Historical data retention policy and archival strategy.
Test cases and acceptance criteria for each sub-FR.
